result:
All three Plan CRDs are well-formed. Here is the complete phase brief and rationale for each decomposition decision:

---

## Phase Brief — `phase-05-heterogeneous-dispatch-demo`

**Objective.** Deliver the operator-facing configuration surface and end-to-end integration demonstration for mixed-provider (Claude + Codex) dispatch. Phases 01–04 ship the Codex Go package, container image, and the per-level vendor switch in the controller. This phase wires the Helm chart to expose those capabilities, closes the `OPENAI_API_KEY` credential path via a schema-additive `CodexProviderSecretRef` field, and exercises the full mixed-provider loop with a reference Project YAML and an offline-safe integration test.

**Scope.** In scope: chart `values.yaml` + `deployment.yaml` (`images.codexSubagent`, `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE`, `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_*` env vars); `cmd/manager/env.go` `resolvePerLevelVendors()` so the Helm vendor defaults flow through `ProviderDefaults` to `ResolveProvider`; `ProjectSpec.CodexProviderSecretRef` API addition wired as a second `envFrom: secretRef` in `podjob/jobspec.go`; a reference heterogeneous example Project YAML; and a mixed-provider integration test reproducing the D4 wave-boundary failure contract. Out of scope: the Codex subagent package (phase 02), Dockerfile/entrypoint (phase 03), `LevelConfig.Vendor` + `ResolveProvider` vendor chain (phase 04).

**Verification gates.** `make test` exits 0 with no existing test regressions; `make verify-import-firewall` + `make verify-dispatch-imports` green; `helm template` renders `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE`, `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_*`, and the `CodexProviderSecretRef` Secret mount cleanly with no inlined credentials; integration test skips offline, reproduces D4 failure semantics when credentials are present, and confirms `tide_cost_cents_total` + cache-efficiency panel carry Codex spend under the locked `{project,phase,plan,wave}` label set (no `task` label).

---

## Plan Decomposition

**Wave 1 — two parallel plans with disjoint file sets:**

- **`plan-01-chart-codex-provider-wiring`** — purely Helm + manager env. Touches `charts/tide/values.yaml`, `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml`, `cmd/manager/env.go`, and `cmd/manager/env_test.go`. No API type changes; no CRD regeneration. Can run alongside plan-02 because the file sets are completely disjoint.

- **`plan-02-codex-secret-injection`** — purely API type + podjob wiring. Touches `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go`, `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`, the CRD manifest, `podjob/jobspec.go`, and `jobspec_test.go`. No chart or env.go changes. The `CodexProviderSecretRef` field is optional (`+optional`, `omitempty`), so it is backwards-compatible — existing Projects without the field are unaffected.

**Wave 2 — single join barrier, depends on both wave-1 plans:**

- **`plan-03-hetero-demo-and-integration-test`** — the reference example Project YAML consumes the chart values added in plan-01 (per-level vendor keys) and the `codexProviderSecretRef` field added in plan-02; the integration test exercises both. Placed in wave 2 to guarantee the example and test reflect the complete, post-plan-01/02 contract rather than a moving target.

spec.objective: Add Helm chart surface for Codex provider: images.codexSubagent block, CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment, TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_* env vars for per-level vendor defaults. Extend cmd/manager/env.go with resolvePerLevelVendors() and update tideHelmProviderDefaults() so vendor defaults reach ResolveProvider. helm template renders all new env vars cleanly; verify-import-firewall and verify-dispatch-imports green; make test exits 0.

Add CodexProviderSecretRef field to ProjectSpec (schema-additive, backwards-compatible) and wire it into internal/dispatch/podjob/jobspec.go as a second envFrom secretRef on dispatch Job pods, mirroring the existing ProviderSecretRef mount. Regenerate CRD manifest and deepcopy. Add jobspec_test.go assertion that CodexProviderSecretRef mounts as envFrom when set and is absent when unset. make manifests and make generate exit 0 with no uncommitted diff; make test exits 0.

Add examples/projects/hetero/project.yaml (reference Claude-planner plus Codex-executor Project using subagent.levels.task.vendor openai, subagent.levels.task.image ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent, and codexProviderSecretRef). Update examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml to consume the new codexProviderSecretRef and per-level vendor fields. Add test/integration/kind/hetero_dispatch_test.go exercising mixed-provider waves: all real OpenAI API calls are guarded by OPENAI_API_KEY env set and testing.Short(); test reproduces D4 failure semantics (forced Codex task failure causes sibling to continue, dependents to never dispatch, non-dependents to dispatch in strict profile); asserts tide_cost_cents_total and cache-efficiency panel carry non-zero Codex spend under locked label set with task label absent. make test exits 0; metriccardinality analyzer green.
